messages.content: You are a Lean 4 autoformalization agent working in a current Mathlib-only project. You must formalize the provided TeX/math source chunk into one Lean theorem or lemma, including a proof, using only the Lean prefix context provided. The target Lean statement, target declaration name, and original proof are intentionally withheld. Avoid stale Lean 3/old Mathlib syntax and identifiers; follow the lean_environment/current_version_guidance and local Lean examples. Return exactly one JSON object.

{
  "task": "Formalize the source chunk as one Lean theorem/lemma and prove it.",
  "required_json_schema": {
    "lean_declaration": "one complete Lean theorem or lemma, including proof/body; do not include imports or markdown",
    "declaration_name": "the local name you gave that theorem/lemma",
    "used_context": [
      "short list of source/context facts used"
    ],
    "notes": [
      "brief caveats, if any"
    ]
  },
  "instructions": [
    "Do not use sorry, admit, aesop? placeholders, or comments standing in for proof.",
    "Do not include import statements; the generated file already imports Mathlib.",
    "Do not assume access to the withheld target Lean statement or name.",
    "For multi-part TeX chunks, formalize only the specified labeled part/source span. Do not conjoin all parts unless the record explicitly asks for the whole multi-part result.",
    "Do not cite or invent raw helper names that are not present in the Lean prefix context/local examples; prefer displayed local style and standard Mathlib APIs.",
    "Do not redeclare definitions, structures, abbrevs, notation helpers, or instances already present in the Lean prefix context; reference them directly.",
    "Do not introduce theorem-local `where` definitions or redefine concepts such as summability, limits, binomial coefficients, or matrix operations. If a needed definition is not in context, state a narrower theorem using the displayed APIs.",
    "Use current Lean 4/Mathlib syntax and API names; if your memory conflicts with displayed local context, trust the displayed context.",
    "State a single proposition-level theorem/lemma that is likely to match the specified source part; do not bundle typeclass instances or unrelated source parts into conjunctions.",
    "Every nonstandard helper theorem or local API used in the proof should appear explicitly in the Lean prefix context or local examples. If it is not displayed, do not use its name.",
    "If an existing theorem or lemma in the prefix context has binders, apply it to the needed variables rather than using the theorem constant bare.",
    "Prefer the narrowest theorem directly supported by the source sentence; avoid generalizing to a stronger forall/if statement unless that exact shape is in the source or prefix context.",
    "Prefer a short proof if the prefix context already contains the needed fact."
  ],
  "context": {
    "source_statement_or_chunk": [
      {
        "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/tex/SymmetricFunctions/Definitions.tex",
        "line_range": [
          276,
          313
        ],
        "original_line_range": [
          277,
          309
        ],
        "line_range_adjustment_reasons": [
          "expanded_backward_to_include_environment_begin",
          "expanded_forward_to_close_environment"
        ],
        "labels": [
          "thm.sf.S-subalg"
        ],
        "snippet": "\\begin{theorem}\n\\label{thm.sf.S-subalg}The subset $\\mathcal{S}$ is a $K$-subalgebra of\n$\\mathcal{P}$.\n\\end{theorem}\n\n\\begin{fineprint}\n\\begin{proof}\n[Proof of Theorem \\ref{thm.sf.S-subalg} (sketched).]We need to show that\n$\\mathcal{S}$ is closed under addition, multiplication and scaling, and that\n$\\mathcal{S}$ contains the zero and the unity of $\\mathcal{P}$. Let me just\nshow that $\\mathcal{S}$ is closed under multiplication (since all the other\nclaims are equally easy): Let $f,g\\in\\mathcal{S}$. We must show that\n$fg\\in\\mathcal{S}$.\n\nThe polynomial $f$ is symmetric (since $f\\in\\mathcal{S}$); in other words,\n$\\sigma\\cdot f=f$ for each $\\sigma\\in S_{N}$. Similarly, $\\sigma\\cdot g=g$ for\neach $\\sigma\\in S_{N}$. Now, from (\\ref{pf.prop.sf.SN-acts-by-alg-auts.1}), we\nsee that%\n\\[\n\\text{for each }\\sigma\\in S_{N}\\text{, we have}\\ \\ \\ \\ \\ \\ \\ \\ \\ \\ \\sigma\n\\cdot\\left(  fg\\right)  =\\underbrace{\\left(  \\sigma\\cdot f\\right)\n}_{\\substack{=f\\\\\\text{(as we have seen)}}}\\cdot\\underbrace{\\left(\n\\sigma\\cdot g\\right)  }_{\\substack{=g\\\\\\text{(as we have seen)}}}=fg.\n\\]\nThis shows that $fg$ is symmetric, i.e., we have $fg\\in\\mathcal{S}$.\n\nForget that we fixed $f,g$. We thus have shown that $fg\\in\\mathcal{S}$ for any\n$f,g\\in\\mathcal{S}$. This shows that $\\mathcal{S}$ is closed under\nmultiplication. As explained above, this concludes our proof of Theorem\n\\ref{thm.sf.S-subalg}.\n\\end{proof}\n\\end{fineprint}\n\n\\begin{definition}\n\\label{def.sf.ring-of-symm}The $K$-subalgebra $\\mathcal{S}$ of $\\mathcal{P}$\nis called the \\emph{ring of symmetric polynomials in }$N$\\emph{ variables over\n}$K$.\n\\end{definition}\n"
      }
    ],

      {
        "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/tex/SymmetricFunctions/Definitions.tex",

        ],

          "We must show that fg S ."

      }
    ],

      ],

      "target_declaration_source_comment": null,

      "context_mode": "source-only",
      "instruction": "If the source snippet contains multiple labeled or numbered parts, formalize only the specific part/source span indicated by visible source labels or explicit focus metadata. Do not conjoin all parts unless the record labels identify the whole multi-part result."
    },
    "context_mode": "source-only",
    "available_imports": [
      "Mathlib",
      "AlgebraicCombinatorics.Permutations.Basics"
    ],
    "lean_prefix_context": "-- File context: AlgebraicCombinatorics/SymmetricFunctions/Definitions.lean:46-46 (open)\nopen scoped Polynomial\n\n-- File context: AlgebraicCombinatorics/SymmetricFunctions/Definitions.lean:47-47 (open)\nopen MvPolynomial Finset\n\n-- File context: AlgebraicCombinatorics/SymmetricFunctions/Definitions.lean:49-49 (namespace)\nnamespace AlgebraicCombinatorics\n\n-- File context: AlgebraicCombinatorics/SymmetricFunctions/Definitions.lean:51-51 (namespace)\nnamespace SymmetricPolynomials\n\n-- File context: AlgebraicCombinatorics/SymmetricFunctions/Definitions.lean:53-53 (variable)\nvariable {K : Type*} [CommRing K]\n\n-- File context: AlgebraicCombinatorics/SymmetricFunctions/Definitions.lean:54-54 (variable)\nvariable {N : ℕ}\n\n-- Predecessor context: AlgebraicCombinatorics/SymmetricFunctions/Definitions.lean:66-66\nabbrev P (K : Type*) [CommRing K] (N : ℕ) : Type _ := MvPolynomial (Fin N) K\n\n-- File context: AlgebraicCombinatorics/SymmetricFunctions/Definitions.lean:84-84 (notation)\nscoped notation:70 σ \" •ₚ \" f:70 => permAction σ f\n\n-- Predecessor context: AlgebraicCombinatorics/SymmetricFunctions/Definitions.lean:131-134\n/-- A polynomial is symmetric if it is invariant under all permutations.\n    This is Definition def.sf.PS (c) in the source.\n    Label: def.sf.PS -/\ndef IsSymm (f : P K N) : Prop := f.IsSymmetric",
    "lean_environment": {
      "toolchain": "leanprover/lean4:v4.28.0",
      "imports": [
        "Mathlib"
      ],
      "current_version_guidance": [
        "Generated code is checked with the repository lean-toolchain and current Mathlib, not Lean 3 or an older Lean 4 snapshot.",
        "Prefer Lean 4 syntax: `fun x => ...`, `by` tactic blocks, namespaces/dot notation as shown in the prompt, and current Mathlib names from displayed context.",
        "Do not invent old/deprecated identifiers such as guessed `*_apply`, `det_swap_rows`, `CommAlgebra`, or `LaurentPolynomial.X` unless they are explicitly present in context; use displayed local APIs or prove by unfolding/simping.",
        "For power-series coefficients, follow the local examples' argument order such as `coeff n (X : R⟦X⟧)`, not `coeff (X : R⟦X⟧) n`.",
        "For Laurent polynomial samples, prefer displayed current APIs such as `LaurentPolynomial.T n` and local notation `K[T;T⁻¹]`; do not use bare polynomial `X` for Laurent polynomials.",
        "Do not make typeclass objects (`CommRing α`, `Algebra R A`, etc.) components of an `∧`; those are types/classes, not propositions. Put them as assumptions/instances or use theorem statements about terms instead.",
        "If the source theorem is a narrow identity, formalize that identity directly rather than a broad bundled theorem whose components will not match the grader's withheld statement."
      ],
      "available_reference_corpus": [
        "Current-version mathlib source can be searched locally at /tmp/mathlib4-v4.28.0-src when preparing/evaluating prompts.",
        "Use nearby repository Lean prefix/local examples first; use mathlib source snippets only as API/style evidence, not as a source for the withheld target statement."
      ]
    },
    "local_lean_style": {
      "guidance": [
        "Match the local Lean style shown in local_lean_style.examples when it applies; prefer exact APIs and argument order already used in this file.",
        "Use only identifiers that appear in the Lean prefix context/local examples or are standard Mathlib identifiers; do not cite or invent raw helper names from guessed notation expansions.",
        "If the local examples use a domain-specific API such as `Ring.choose`, `updateCol`, or a local theorem name, keep that exact API family instead of falling back to a more familiar but different API such as `Nat.choose` or `Matrix.updateColumn`."
      ],
      "notation_contracts": [],
      "examples": [
        "In Mathlib, this is given by `MvPolynomial.rename σ f` where σ : Equiv.Perm (Fin N).\n-/\n\n/-- The action of a permutation on a polynomial by renaming variables.\n    This is σ · f = f[x_{σ(1)}, ..., x_{σ(N)}] in the source.\n    Label: def.sf.PS -/\nnoncomputable def permAction (σ : Equiv.Perm (Fin N)) (f : P K N) : P K N :=\n  rename σ f\n\n/-- Notation: σ • f for the permutation action -/\nscoped notation:70 σ \" •ₚ \" f:70 => permAction σ f\n\n/-!\n## Proposition prop.sf.SN-acts: The action is a well-defined group action\n\n(a) id · f = f for every f ∈ P\n(b) (στ) · f = σ · (τ · f) for every σ, τ ∈ S_N and f ∈ P\n-/\n\n/-- The identity permutation acts trivially (Proposition prop.sf.SN-acts (a)).\n    Label: prop.sf.SN-acts -/\ntheorem permAction_id (f : P K N) : (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin N)) •ₚ f = f := by\n  simp only [permAction, Equiv.Perm.coe_one, rename_id]\n  rfl\n\n/-- Composition of permutation actions (Proposition prop.sf.SN-acts (b)).\n    Label: prop.sf.SN-acts -/\ntheorem permAction_mul (σ τ : Equiv.Perm (Fin N)) (f : P K N) :\n    (σ * τ) •ₚ f = σ •ₚ (τ •ₚ f) := by\n  simp only [permAction, rename_rename]\n  rfl\n\n/-- The symmetric group action on polynomials is a well-defined MulAction.\n    This formalizes Proposition prop.sf.SN-acts in the standard Mathlib form:\n    - `one_smul` corresponds to part (a): id · f = f"
      ]
    },
    "domain_statement_shape_guidance": [],
    "mathlib_context": [
      "Mathlib APIs referenced by imported modules; exact proof-level facts not statically certified."
    ],
    "benchmark_policy": {
      "target_lean_statement_available_to_model": false,
      "target_declaration_name_available_to_model": false,
      "grading_uses_withheld_gold_statement": true,
      "source_only_removed_hidden_target_context_blocks": 0
    }
  }
}
